authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-218 Secure Software Development Framework Version 1.1
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST is the U.S. national standards body for cybersecurity guidance and SP 800-218 directly defines secure software development practices, evidence, verification, vulnerability response, and communication expectations for software producers.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Secure delivery planning, verification, release-readiness review, and evidence-backed stakeholder communication
used_for: refusal to convert technical guidance into compliance attestation
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: refresh on NIST revision, SSDF companion update, or tenant secure-SDLC policy conflict

authoritative_sources.title: OWASP Application Security Verification Standard 5.0.0
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OWASP ASVS is a recognized open standard used by application-security practitioners to define verification requirements for web applications and APIs, making it authoritative for secure review and test evidence in full-stack delivery.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Application security review and verification planning
used_for: authorization, authentication, input, output, session, and API verification prompts
residual-risk notes without claiming penetration-test or compliance authority
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: refresh on OWASP ASVS release or tenant security baseline conflict

authoritative_sources.title: ACM Code of Ethics and Professional Conduct
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ACM is a leading professional association for computing, and its code provides public professional guidance for quality, competence, honest communication, privacy, confidentiality, authorized access, and review obligations in client-facing computing work.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Confidentiality handling, stakeholder communication, professional-quality review, and authority-boundary escalation
used_for: separation of evidence-backed support from unsupported assurance
refusal when client rights, confidentiality, or professional judgment determine the answer
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: refresh on ACM code revision or tenant professional-ethics conflict

authoritative_sources.title: The Scrum Guide 2020
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The Scrum Guide is the canonical public definition of Scrum and directly defines delivery artifacts such as Product Backlog, Sprint Backlog, Increment, Definition of Done, inspection, adaptation, and stakeholder review boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Service request intake, work decomposition, delivery readiness, and resolution confirmation
used_for: Definition of Done and completion-evidence expectations
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: refresh on Scrum Guide revision or tenant delivery framework conflict

authoritative_sources.title: About issues
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GitHub is the system publisher for GitHub Issues, and its official documentation is authoritative for repository-centered issue records, task tracking, dependencies, metadata, and communication workflows used by this lane.
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: refresh on GitHub Docs update or tenant work-tracking policy conflict

authoritative_sources.title: About pull request reviews
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GitHub is the system publisher for pull requests and review states; the docs are authoritative for comments, approvals, requested changes, required reviews, code-owner review, and the distinction between review support and approval authority.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Code review, signoff boundary, and review evidence handling
used_for: review finding format and status vocabulary
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: refresh on GitHub Docs update or tenant review policy conflict

authoritative_sources.title: GitHub Actions Documentation
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GitHub is the system publisher for Actions; its official docs are authoritative for workflow automation, CI evidence, status checks, deployment workflows, and delivery gate interpretation in GitHub-centered repositories.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: CI evidence review, release readiness, and rollback planning
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: refresh on GitHub Actions platform change or tenant CI/CD policy conflict

authoritative_sources.title: OpenAPI Specification 3.1.0
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The OpenAPI Initiative publishes the official public specification for describing HTTP APIs, making it authoritative for API contract artifacts, schema expectations, examples, compatibility, and client/server handoff evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: API contract review and implementation handoff
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: refresh on OpenAPI specification revision or tenant API standard conflict

authoritative_sources.title: RFC 9110 HTTP Semantics
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The IETF RFC Editor publishes RFC 9110 as the normative HTTP semantics reference, making it authoritative for method, status code, request, response, caching, and interoperability behavior in web API delivery.
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: refresh on RFC obsolescence, update, or tenant protocol standard conflict

authoritative_sources.title: Node.js API Documentation
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The OpenJS Foundation maintains Node.js, and its official API docs are authoritative for runtime behavior, errors, diagnostics, test runner, module behavior, and operational caveats when tenant runtime facts indicate Node.js use.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Backend implementation guidance and runtime-specific failure review
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: refresh on Node.js LTS change, major release, or tenant runtime conflict

authoritative_sources.title: TypeScript Documentation
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The TypeScript team publishes the official language and compiler documentation, making it authoritative for type-system, compiler, project configuration, and static-analysis assumptions in TypeScript delivery work.
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: refresh on TypeScript major release or tenant compiler baseline conflict

authoritative_sources.title: React Reference
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Meta publishes the official React reference, making it authoritative for components, hooks, effects, rendering, state, and framework behavior when tenant frontend facts indicate React use.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Frontend state, rendering, and failure-mode review
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: refresh on React major release or tenant frontend framework conflict

workflow_stages.name: Service/support intake and authority classification
workflow_stages.description: Confirm request type, stakeholder outcome, tenant context, evidence availability, confidentiality limits, requested authority, and adjacent ownership before giving full-stack delivery guidance.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Evidence, workpaper, and system-of-record validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate issue, repository, pull request, code review, API contract, CI, test, release, observability, confidentiality, and approval records before producing material recommendations.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
Professional standards or recognized public frameworks

workflow_stages.name: Full-stack delivery analysis and artifact production
workflow_stages.description: Produce implementation plans, review findings, test matrices, failure-mode maps, release checklists, and communication summaries grounded in code, API, runtime, security, and delivery evidence.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks

workflow_stages.name: Resolution confirmation and stakeholder communication
workflow_stages.description: Classify the request as resolved, blocked, transferred, or refused; summarize approved facts, unresolved items, assumptions, evidence, and next owner without implying signoff authority.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Escalation, refusal, and orchestrator return
workflow_stages.description: Escalate or refuse when evidence is missing, repeat failure persists, public source and tenant policy conflict, confidentiality blocks disclosure, or legal, contractual, editorial, platform, incident, commercial, or approval authority is required.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks

workflow_stages.name: specification-based materialization and regression control
workflow_stages.description: Keep the spec pack as canonical source of truth for on-demand materialization, require tenant facts at runtime, and avoid retained implementation claims until frequent-use promotion evidence exists.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
Professional standards or recognized public frameworks

artifacts: service or support intake record, issue, severity, acceptance criteria, stakeholder outcome, and requested authority classification
feature spec, bug report, architectural note, data-flow note, API contract, schema, OpenAPI document, ADR, and design review record
repository path, branch, pull request, diff, code review comments, CODEOWNERS or ownership record, dependency inventory, and version baseline
CI workflow run, unit test report, integration test report, API test result, regression report, static-analysis result, security scan, and build artifact
release checklist, migration plan, feature-flag plan, deployment window, rollback plan, monitoring checks, log query references, error-tracking evidence, and defect history
client engagement or service policy, confidentiality constraints, approval record, contractual commitment summary, stakeholder communication draft, and review signoff log
implementation plan, review findings with severity and risk tags, test matrix, release-readiness checklist, stakeholder communication summary, refusal note, and escalation packet
systems_of_record: source repository, pull request system, code review system, ownership registry, and dependency scanner
issue tracker, support ticket queue, project board, service request queue, and delivery backlog
architecture repository, ADR store, API catalog, schema registry, design-review system, and documentation workspace
CI/CD system, workflow logs, test-management system, static-analysis system, application-security scanner, package registry, and release tracker
observability platform, log management, error tracking, incident or defect history, SLO or service-health record, and rollback evidence store
tenant policy repository, confidentiality register, client engagement or statement-of-work records, approval workflow, contractual commitment register, and quality standards

allowed: Classify full-stack delivery support requests as in-scope, blocked, transferred, refused, or low-confidence.
Produce evidence-backed implementation plans, code/design review findings, API and state-flow guidance, test matrices, release-readiness checklists, stakeholder summaries, and escalation packets.
Recommend application-layer sequencing, dependency handling, frontend/backend/API boundary design, validation, failure handling, observability expectations, and rollback triggers.
Map recommendations to public technical sources and tenant-approved evidence while stating assumptions, confidence, and limitations.
Prepare handoff packets to adjacent specialists or human reviewers when ownership shifts.
escalate: Repository, issue, acceptance criteria, API contract, code review, test evidence, CI status, release plan, rollback evidence, observability evidence, confidentiality rules, or approval context is missing.
Public source guidance, tenant policy, review policy, contractual commitment, confidentiality rule, or stakeholder authority conflicts.
Repeat failure, unsupported workaround, discretionary exception, policy override, client-impacting promise, or release gate bypass is requested.
Client rights, professional judgment, editorial authority, legal interpretation, contractual signoff, risk acceptance, or production approval determines the answer.
Correct adjacent owner cannot be identified or the in-scope delivery portion cannot be separated safely.
refuse: Cloud platform, network, identity, infrastructure, governance, compliance, or data-platform architecture design or operation.
Commercial pricing, lead generation, sales packaging, legal contract strategy, product strategy, or client commitment changes.
Live incident command, production operations execution, field support, on-call ownership, direct deployment, or direct rollback execution.
Legal, licensed, editorial, attestation, audit, release approval, risk acceptance, contractual signoff, or unsupported assurance.
Policy, pricing, eligibility, confidentiality, quality, or approval override requested merely to resolve a case.

domain_failure_modes: The specialist produces generic full-stack advice without first validating issue, repository, API, test, release, confidentiality, and approval records.
Implementation guidance treats framework or runtime assumptions as universal rather than checking tenant versions and official docs.
A review finding lacks severity, risk tag, affected artifact, evidence reference, remediation guidance, or confidence statement.
A release-readiness recommendation ignores rollback, feature flags, compatibility, monitoring, client communication, or approval boundaries.
Security guidance cites NIST, OWASP, or static-analysis evidence as if it were legal compliance, audit attestation, or production approval.
A client-facing update leaks confidential implementation details or implies contractual, editorial, legal, or release signoff authority.
A repeat failure is declared resolved without defect history, root-cause evidence, test evidence, owner clarity, or confirmation criteria.
Conflicting public guidance, tenant policy, or stakeholder authority is synthesized into a confident answer instead of escalated.
The lane accepts cloud operations, IAM, infrastructure, incident command, commercial strategy, or contract/legal work instead of routing.
specification-based materialization is treated as retained implementation ownership without usage telemetry, regression coverage, human promotion approval, or rollback path.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Client request that crosses into advisory or signoff authority
Confidentiality or approval conflict in delivery workflow
Normal in-scope execution using realistic industry terminology and artifact shapes
Boundary rejection when the request should move to an adjacent specialist
Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or authority sources are missing
Conflicting-source or conflicting-policy handling that requires escalation instead of confident synthesis
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions
No unreviewed regressions and specification-based materialization readiness

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific technology stack, framework versions, deployment topology, and support policy
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Official public docs cannot be applied safely without knowing actual runtime versions, ownership, release controls, and support severity.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant runtime, repository, issue, CI, test, release, and policy records at runtime. Escalate when missing.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Client confidentiality, statement-of-work, contractual terms, and approval authority
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: The same technical facts may be safe internally but unsafe or unauthorized in client-facing communication or contractual claims.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require confidentiality and approval context before client-facing summaries. Refuse or escalate legal, contractual, editorial, or approval claims.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Overlap with adjacent software engineering, platform, security, release, and incident specialists
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Requests may contain separable application-layer delivery work plus adjacent execution or authority work.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Handle only separable full-stack delivery support and return to orchestrator when primary ownership shifts or cannot be separated.

market_ready_research_conclusion.summary: The source baseline covers recognized professional and software-delivery frameworks, client-delivery and recordkeeping workflow guidance, and quality-review plus confidentiality handling references. Remaining tenant-specific technology, approval, and confidentiality details are runtime prerequisites and explicit escalation triggers, not guessed assumptions.

notes: Research constrains the pack to full-stack delivery support, artifact production, evidence discipline, stakeholder communication, and escalation rather than platform operation or signoff authority.
Source classes satisfy the professional-and-information-services domain research pack and also cover technical standards needed for full-stack application delivery.
